description: <onTimeout> misplaced outside its owning locus (§51.0.M / §4.15). <onTimeout> is a structural element grammatical only inside an <engine> state-child body; placed inside a ${ } logic body it is misplaced and fires E-STRUCTURAL-ELEMENT-MISPLACED (the §34 error partition). NOTE (flagged to PA): <onTimeout> in PLAIN <program> markup (a different 'outside' locus) fires E-ATTR-001 + E-SCOPE-001 rather than E-STRUCTURAL-ELEMENT-MISPLACED — an error still fires (fail-closed), but the specific code diverges from §51.0.M's blanket 'outside an engine state-child -> E-STRUCTURAL-ELEMENT-MISPLACED'. This case uses the ${}-logic-body locus, which fires the SPEC code exactly.
rationale: §51.0.M <onTimeout> is legal only inside an <engine> state-child body; used outside its owning locus -> E-STRUCTURAL-ELEMENT-MISPLACED (§4.15 structural-element placement gate).
